Title: Introgression of SbERD4 Gene Encodes an Early-Responsive Dehydration-Stress Protein That Confers Tolerance against Different Types of Abiotic Stresses in Transgenic Tobacco

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SbERD4 cDNA is 2367 bp, comprising a 5'-UTR (1–64 bp), an ORF (65–2191 bp), and a 3'-UTR (2192–2367 bp), encoding a 708-amino-acid protein. The gene shows high sequence similarity to CSC1-like ERD4 proteins from several glycophytes (e.g., Spinacia oleracea, Beta vulgaris, Chenopodium quinoa). SbERD4 is classified as an early-responsive to dehydration (ERD) gene and is part of the broader ERD gene family originally defined in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SbERD4 from the extreme halophyte Salicornia brachiata encodes a CSC1-like membrane protein with 10 transmembrane domains and localizes to the plasma membrane.
2. SbERD4 transcript in S. brachiata is strongly and rapidly induced by multiple abiotic stresses (salt, osmotic/drought, cold, and heat), with up to ~20-fold up-regulation.
3. Constitutive overexpression of SbERD4 in tobacco significantly enhances tolerance to salinity, osmotic/drought, cold, and heat stresses, improving seed germination, growth, relative water content, membrane stability, and reducing electrolyte leakage.
4. SbERD4 overexpression promotes accumulation of osmoprotectants (proline, soluble sugars, starch, polyphenols, free amino acids) and maintains favorable K+/Na+ ratio and lower Na+ accumulation under salt stress.
5. SbERD4 overexpression reduces H2O2 accumulation and is associated with strong up-regulation of antioxidant enzyme genes (NtSOD, NtAPX, NtCAT, NtGR) under stress, indicating enhanced ROS-scavenging capacity.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of SbERD4 enhances plant tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses (salinity, osmotic/drought, cold, heat) by improving growth, water status, membrane stability, osmoprotectant accumulation, ion homeostasis, and antioxidant defense, resulting in reduced oxidative and cellular damage under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: SbERD4 is a plasma membrane CSC1-like, Ca-dependent channel-like protein with 10 transmembrane domains; its overexpression is associated with maintenance of ionic balance (higher K+, lower Na+, lower Na+/K+ ratio), enhanced accumulation of osmoprotectants, and transcriptional up-regulation of antioxidant enzyme genes (NtSOD, NtAPX, NtCAT, NtGR), thereby reinforcing ROS-scavenging and cell homeostasis under abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: SbERD4 -->(activation; overexpression-dependent)--> NtSOD, NtAPX, NtCAT, NtGR (transcriptional up-regulation 2–6-fold under salt, osmotic/drought, cold, and heat stress in transgenic tobacco, associated with enhanced ROS scavenging).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CSC1-like ERD4 protein (708 amino acids) with a predicted Ca-dependent channel-like transmembrane domain architecture comprising 10 transmembrane domains.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane, demonstrated by SbERD4:RFP fusion transiently expressed in onion epidermal cells showing fluorescence confined to the plasma membrane compared with diffuse RFP signal in vector control cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In S. brachiata, SbERD4 is strongly induced by abiotic stresses: up to ~20-fold under 500 mM NaCl, ~15-fold under 300 mM mannitol (osmotic/drought), and ~10-fold under 40°C heat at 24 h; ~16-fold induction at 3 h under 4°C cold; expression declines by 48 h under all stresses.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salinity stress (200 mM and 500 mM NaCl); osmotic/drought stress (300 mM mannitol); cold stress (4°C); heat stress (40°C); treatments applied to S. brachiata seedlings (up to 48 h) and to transgenic tobacco plants in hydroponic and soil conditions.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance, including salinity stress, osmotic/drought stress, cold stress, heat stress; osmotic adjustment and oxidative stress tolerance via improved ion homeostasis and ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under salt or osmotic stress, SbERD4-overexpressing tobacco seeds showed ~70% germination vs 40–45% in controls; under stress, transgenic seedlings exhibited greater shoot length (0.5–0.6 cm vs 0.35 cm), root length (4.5–5.0 cm vs 2.5–3.0 cm), fresh weight (15–16 mg vs 10–11 mg), and dry weight (1.8–1.9 mg vs 1.0 mg). Under multiple stresses, RWC increased to ~58–60% (salt), 54–55% (osmotic), 59% (cold), 51–52% (heat) in transgenics vs 43–48% in controls; MSI was ~0.9 (salt/osmotic) and 0.7–0.8 (cold/heat) in transgenics vs 0.6–0.7 in controls; EL decreased to 4–5% vs 6–7% in controls. Under salt stress, Na+ content remained ~0.06–0.08 mg g^-1 DW in transgenics vs ~0.2 mg g^-1 DW in controls, while K+ increased to ~0.25 mg g^-1 DW in transgenics vs ~0.1 mg g^-1 DW in controls, resulting in a higher K+/Na+ ratio. Osmoprotectants such as proline, total soluble sugars, polyphenols, reducing sugars, and free amino acids accumulated to markedly higher levels in transgenics than in controls under all stresses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Salicornia brachiata seedlings for SbERD4 expression profiling; Nicotiana tabacum cv. Petit Havana transgenic lines (L2, L13, L14, L15, L18) overexpressing SbERD4, with wild-type and vector-control tobacco plants for functional assays; onion epidermal cells for SbERD4:RFP subcellular localization.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: EST-based cloning and RACE to obtain full-length SbERD4 cDNA; sequence analysis and submission to NCBI; in silico prediction of transmembrane domains and conserved domains; SbERD4:RFP fusion construct and transient transformation of onion epidermal cells for subcellular localization; qRT-PCR of SbERD4 in S. brachiata under salt, mannitol, cold, and heat stresses; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of Nicotiana tabacum (cv. Petit Havana) with pCAMBIA1301::35SSbERD4; PCR and GUS histochemical assay to confirm transgenics; seed germination and early growth assays under NaCl and mannitol; leaf disc senescence and chlorophyll quantification; hydroponic and soil-based stress treatments (salt, mannitol, cold, heat); measurements of RWC, MSI, electrolyte leakage, osmoprotectants (proline, sugars, starch, polyphenols, free amino acids), H2O2 content; Na+ and K+ ion content analysis; qRT-PCR of ROS-scavenging genes (NtSOD, NtAPX, NtCAT, NtGR); statistical analysis (ANOVA, Tukey’s HSD).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of SbERD4 in Nicotiana tabacum cv. Petit Havana followed by multi-stress (salt, mannitol, cold, heat) tolerance assays (germination, growth, RWC, MSI, EL, osmoprotectants, ion content, H2O2) and qRT-PCR analysis of antioxidant genes; subcellular localization by SbERD4:RFP fusion in onion epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: Controlled soil-based experiments in a greenhouse: 28-day-old transgenic and control tobacco plants were transplanted to soil and irrigated with 200 mM NaCl or 300 mM mannitol on alternate days for 15 days, followed by recovery under normal irrigation to assess growth and stress performance.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: SbERD4 was obtained from an EST clone (Sal C38; GenBank EC906161) from S. brachiata and made full length using RACE with gene-specific primers, followed by cloning, sequencing, and functional validation via transgenic overexpression in tobacco.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: SbERD4, derived from the extreme halophyte Salicornia brachiata, confers robust tolerance to salinity, osmotic/drought, cold, and heat stresses when overexpressed in tobacco, improving growth, water status, ion homeostasis, and ROS detoxification. These features make SbERD4 a promising candidate gene for engineering multi-abiotic-stress tolerance in crop plants, particularly for cultivation on saline or drought-prone soils, thereby supporting stable yield and resilience under climate-change-associated stress conditions.
